headline: New bank capital requirements, Five Below surges over Q4 holiday results
summary: Yahoo Finance Host Josh Lipton tracks today's top moving stocks and biggest market stories in this Market Minute: As strikes on power facilities in the Middle East intensify, Wall Street sees renewed concerns around inflation and dampening hope for interest rate cuts. A proposal to lower bank capital requirements has large bank stocks like JPMorgan Chase (JPM) and Bank of America (BAC) ticking slightly lower in Thursday's session. Five Below (FIVE) shares surge following a successful Q4 holiday season.

Five Below (FIVE) shares surge following a successful Q4 holiday season.

headline: Nexstar Readies High-Grade Bond Sale to Fund $6 Billion Takeover
summary: Bank of America Corp. has indicated to investors that Nexstar will get a second investment-grade rating from Fitch Ratings, allowing the potential high-grade bond offering to proceed, according to people familiar with the matter.  The Irving, Texas-based TV station operator is also weighing selling high-yield unsecured notes as part of the financing package, the people added.

raw.summary: Bank of America Corp. has indicated to investors that Nexstar will get a second investment-grade rating from Fitch Ratings, allowing the potential high-grade bond offering to proceed, according to people familiar with the matter. headline: BofA Teams with Visa to Offer Cardholders a Chance to Win a Trip to the FIFA World Cup 2026™
summary: Bank of America, the Official Bank Sponsor of FIFA World Cup 2026™, in partnership with Visa®, the Official Payment Provider of FIFA World Cup 2026™, today announced a sweepstakes for Bank of America Consumer and Small Business Visa® cardholders that provides an opportunity to win one of three hospitality packages to FIFA World Cup 2026™ knockout round matches, simply by making everyday purchases with their eligible credit card.

headline: Trump signals DOJ should continue Powell probe, complicating Warsh Fed nom
summary: Powell has accused the DOJ of investigating him in retaliation for his refusal to lower interest rates as much or as quickly as Trump has demanded.

headline: The Iran war is threatening supply of a little-thought-of resource — helium. What it means for markets
summary: Supply allocations are being set by who needs the gas the most. Semiconductors are at the "top of the pecking order," said helium consultant Phil Kornbluth.
